samples.xml_description:
Implement an asynchronous FIFO, FIFO bit width and depth can be configured(parameter DEPTH = 16, parameter WIDTH = 8). The asynchronous FIFO structure is divided into several parts. The first part is dual-port RAM, which is used for data storage. Instantiate dual-port RAM as a submodule, The RAM ports are input wclk, input wenc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] waddr, input [WIDTH-1:0] wdata, input rclk, input renc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] raddr, output reg [WIDTH-1:0] rdata. The second part is the data write controller. The third part is the data read controller. The fourth part is the read pointer synchronizer. The read pointer is collected using the two-stage trigger of the write clock and output to the data write controller. The fifth part is the write pointer synchronizer, which uses the two-stage trigger of the read clock to collect the write pointer and output it to the data read controller. Write clock. Read clock. Reset signal. Write increment. Read increment. Write data. Write full. Read empty. Read data. Data width of FIFO. Depth of FIFO.

Asynchronous FIFO is composed of four parts. First is dual-port RAM. The FIFO data is stored in the RAM. Instantiate dual-port RAM as a submodule. The RAM ports are input wclk, input wenc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] waddr, input [WIDTH-1:0] wdata, input rclk, input renc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] raddr, output reg [WIDTH-1:0] rdata.

Second part is data write controller. It contains write pointer, write full, write address and write increment. The write pointer is incremented by write increment. When the FIFO is full, the write pointer is not incremented. The write pointer is converted to Gray code. The write pointer is stored in register wptr, which is output to the dual-port RAM. The write pointer is also compared with read pointer to determine if the FIFO is full.

Third part is data read controller. It contains read pointer, read empty, read address and read increment. The read pointer is incremented by read increment. When the FIFO is empty, the read pointer is not incremented. The read pointer is converted to Gray code. The read pointer is stored in register rptr, which is output to the dual-port RAM. The read pointer is also compared with write pointer to determine if the FIFO is empty.

Fourth part is read pointer synchronizer. It contains two-stage trigger of read clock. The read pointer is collected by two-stage trigger of read clock. The read pointer is stored in register rptr_buff, which is output to the data write controller.

Fifth part is write pointer synchronizer. It contains two-stage trigger of write clock. The write pointer is collected by two-stage trigger of write clock. The write pointer is stored in register wptr_buff, which is output to the data read controller.

Full and empty signals are determined by comparing the write pointer and read pointer. When the write pointer equals to the bitwise negation of the most significant bit of the read pointer concatenated with the remaining bits of the read pointer, the FIFO is full. When the read pointer equals to the write pointer, the FIFO is empty.
